steps.title: Find actual distance represented by original map
steps.content: The original map’s side length is 30 inches, and each inch represents 17 miles, so the actual distance represented by the whole side is $$30 \times 17 = 510$$ miles.

steps.title: Compute side length of smaller map
steps.content: The smaller map’s side length is 65% shorter, meaning it retains only 35% of the original: $$30 \times (1 - 0.65) = 30 \times 0.35 = 10.5$$ inches.

steps.title: Determine miles per inch on smaller map
steps.content: The same actual distance (510 miles) is now shown on 10.5 inches, so one inch on the smaller map represents $$\frac{510}{10.5} \approx 48.57$$ miles. That matches choice D — D is correct.
